channel.title: summarizedStoriesMainNLhealthAndWellness
channel.description: This is a randomly ordered feed of the most recent entries from summarizedStoriesMainNLhealthAndWellness, skipping any feeds with errors.

item.title: Spinal cord stimulation may improve arm function years after stroke
item.description: University of Pittsburgh researchers report final results of a pilot clinical trial using electrical stimulation of the spinal cord.

item.title: Nearly seven million children live in homes with unsecured guns
item.description: A Northeastern University study in JAMA Network Open finds guns are the leading cause of death for children in the U.S.

item.title: Global Lewy Body Dementia Incidence: New Data
item.description: The first pooled worldwide analysis shows incidence of dementia with Lewy bodies is higher than that of other uncommon dementias.

item.title: Could a Urine Test Accurately Diagnose Autism?
item.description: A pilot study finds a urine-based metabolite panel accurately distinguishes children with autism from typically developing peers, but larger studies are needed.
